data.title: Centerra Gold's Mount Milligan Mine in BC approved for operations until 2035; 10% plant expansion, expedited permit.
data.description: Centerra Gold Inc. has announced that its Mount Milligan Mine in central British Columbia has been granted an amended environmental assessment and all necessary permits to operate until 2035. The authorizations also include a 10% increase in plant throughput starting in 2028 and expanded stockpile capacity for plant feed flexibility. In January 2025, Mount Milligan was chosen by the Province of British Columbia as one of four mining projects eligible for expedited permitting to boost economic growth in the region.

data.title: Honda and Princeton NuEnergy sign MOU to advance collaboration in next-gen battery recycling tech on Jan 5, 2026.
data.description: Honda and Princeton NuEnergy have signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) on 05-Jan-2026 to enhance their partnership in developing innovative battery recycling technologies. The announcement was made by Princeton NuEnergy through PR Newswire. This collaboration signifies a significant step forward in the advancement of next-generation battery recycling. The MOU aims to focus on research and development efforts to improve sustainability practices in the automotive industry. The alliance between Honda and Princeton NuEnergy is expected to bring about groundbreaking advancements in this sector.

data.title: Appotronics reveals first Laser+LCOS prototype for AR glasses at SPIE Photonics West 2026. #innovation #technology
data.description: Appotronics, a leading provider of ALPD® laser technology, recently introduced the inaugural Laser+LCOS optical engine prototype for Augmented Reality (AR) glasses at the SPIE Photonics West 2026 AR | VR | MR Exhibition in San Francisco on January 20, 2026. This groundbreaking demonstration showcases cutting-edge innovation in AR technology and was met with enthusiasm from attendees.

data.title: Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP, Dogecoin prices fall amidst Trump tariff threats; BTC -3.95% to $88,951.35, ETH -6.76% to $2,965.89, XRP -3.42% to $1.91, Doge -2.23% to $0.1254. Crypto market in 'Extreme Fear', $1.7B liquidated, Bitcoin trading volume surged 76%, Ethereum drops below $3,000.
data.description: On Tuesday, leading cryptocurrencies fell due to President Trump's tariff threats causing a market downturn. Bitcoin dropped by 3.95% to $88,951.35, Ethereum plunged 6.76% to $2,965.89, XRP decreased 3.42% to $1.91, Solana fell by 4.57% to $127.45, and Dogecoin slipped 2.23% to $0.1254. The crypto market experienced extreme fear as Bitcoin saw increased trading volume of 76% and dropped below $88,000. Ethereum also fell below $3,000 for the first time in three weeks, with XRP and Dogecoin witnessing sharp declines. The sell-off affected cryptocurrency-related stocks as well, with MicroStrategy Inc. and Coinbase Global Inc. closing down by 7.76% and 5.57%, respectively. Approximately $1.7 billion was liquidated in the last 24 hours, leading to the disappearance of leveraged longs worth a billion. Bitcoin's open interest decreased by 1.22%, and funds locked in Ethereum futures fell by 3.22%. Market sentiment worsened from "Fear" to "Extreme Fear."
South Korean leader Lee notes North Korea has strengthened the inter-Korean border like never before.
South Korea’s President Lee says North Korea’s distrust of the South is currently at a high.
